extractions.overview: The Data Platform Project is a controlled initiative established by a charter dated February 17, 2026. It is in Draft status, currently In Review (sent to ITS Leads 2/18). The project is part of a broader technology program including CRMPlatform work, but this charter covers the Data Platform Project only. Its purpose is to address fragmented, system-specific reporting by consolidating student data into a single DataCloud data platform, enabling self-service reporting via VizBoard dashboards, establishing trusted "gold" data models, delivering the Analytics report backlog, improving data quality, and supporting the institution's NextGenSIS migration (target Fall 2027 go-live). The project focuses on integrating data from LegacySIS, CampusPro, CRMPlatform, AdmitTrack, HousingDB, and FinAidSys into a medallion architecture data platform, using PipelineTool, CloudStore, and DataPipe for ETL, and providing 19 reports/data sources. It also includes NextGenSIS integration planning, Pipeline monitoring, and infrastructure using GitHub, GitHub Actions, and SchemaSync. Changes to scope, milestones, or governance require a formal change request.
extractions.stakeholders:
- Draft — Version Author
- Jordan Rivera — Project Manager
- Program Lead — Leads this project and the related CRMPlatform project; program-level coordination
- Independent Consultant — Lead Architect
- Director of Enterprise Tech — Technical leadership; manages all technical resources; resource allocation decisions
- David Park — Technical Lead / Data Engineer
- Sarah Mitchell — LegacySIS/Reporting SME
- Analytics team member — Data Analyst
- QA team member — Analyst / Tester
- New BA — BA (Phase 2) (as of Jan 2026)
- New Dev — Developer (Phase 2) (as of Jan 2026)
- Program Sponsors — Executive visibility, strategic direction, change request approval
- Analytics Team Lead — Primary data consumer; defines and prioritizes report requirements; validates and accepts deliverables
extractions.timeline:
- February 17, 2026 — Project Charter Date (estimates based on this date)
- February 18, 2026 — Charter sent to ITS Leads
- Mar 2026 — Core SIS Reports Validated (College & Conservatory All Active Reports delivered and accepted)
- Apr 2026 — Phase 2 Source Ingestion Complete (HousingDB & FinAidSys data ingested and validated at Bronze layer)
- Apr 2026 — Registration/Enrollment Reports Delivered (College & Conservatory Registration/Enrollment w/ Demographics)
- End of Apr 2026 — NextGenSIS Technical POC Complete (Ingestion proof of concept validated end-to-end)
- May 2026 — NTR Financial Reports Delivered (College & Conservatory Net Tuition Revenue reports)
- Jun 2026 — Current-State Scope Freeze** (All current-state reports delivered and platform stabilized)
- End of Jun 2026 — NextGenSIS Data Model Approved** (Data model reviewed and approved, aligned with NextGenSIS Scope Freeze)
- Mar 10, 2026 — NextGenSIS Discovery Complete (Data mapping and technical approach defined)
- TBD (Jun 2026) — Remaining Core Reports Delivered (Restrictions and Graduation reports)
- Fall 2027 — NextGenSIS Go-Live Target
- TBD 2027 — M10* (Rebuilt data model on NextGenSIS sources; Analytics reports migrated)
extractions.risks:
- Key-person dependency (LegacySIS SME) — High severity; Sarah Mitchell is a single point of failure for LegacySIS institutional knowledge
- Architect capacity — High severity; Lead Architect spans all workstreams; Phase 1 delays compress NextGenSIS time
- All Active schedule delay (cascading) — High severity; College All Active validation gates all downstream Analytics reports
- Legacy technical debt — High severity; Platform surfaces pre-existing data quality issues, increasing validation burden
- NextGenSIS/Platform schedule misalignment — High severity; Current-state and future-state work compete for the same resources and timeline
extractions.open_questions:
- Production and test environments need to be established as Phase 1 nears completion (Constraint C3)
- Remaining Core Reports (Restrictions and Graduation reports) delivery date TBD (Jun 2026)
- The M10 milestone for NextGenSIS Data Platform Operational* is TBD 2027, dependent on NextGenSIS go-live (Fall 2027)

conflicts.existing:
- Jaskaran Singh successfully obtained Workday access and is beginning to familiarize himself with the system.
- PERSON_9 completed the archival procedure, which includes archiving daily files for the last 3 days and setting up email notifications upon completion of the archival process.
The project is currently in an 'unmasking' phase, revealing accumulated technical debt previously hidden by manual spreadsheet patches. This phase is crucial for a successful NextGenSIS transition but has led to cascading logic discrepancies.
- A strategic shift has been initiated toward Workflow-Driven Discovery (NextGenSIS Pivot) to ensure a future-state model that is auditable and autonomous. This discovery phase is a current priority.
A strategic shift to 'Workflow-Driven Discovery' has been prioritized to build an auditable, autonomous future-state model.

| R-NEW | Changes to 'check-in restriction messages' and 'registration restriction messages' datasets (changing from description to code-only output by GPE_5 today) will cause issues with existing Snowflake loading processes due to column order and field processing changes. | High | High | Pause affected Snowpipes, update loading processes, perform new bulk load when data is used. | Jaskaran Singh | Open |
| R-NEW | Overloading Informer production systems (e.g., registrations, term enrollment) if large data files are used for API testing | Medium | High | Use smaller files for PoC and explore staggered imports for larger data sets | PERSON_11 | Open |
| R-NEW | Inability to deliver 'hold codes' filter for registration may necessitate splitting the delivery into two different updates, delaying full functionality. | High | High | Obtain decision from PERSON_23 by tomorrow. | PERSON_2 | Open |
| R-NEW | Workday PII masking and other security aspects require further alignment with ORG_88 team, and their understanding of project discovery is currently limited. | Medium | High | Facilitate alignment and share discovery with ORG_88 team. | Sean | Open |
- Single Point of Failure: Project progress is critically tied to the specialized legacy knowledge of a single SME, creating an urgent resource risk (#3).
- Architectural Throughput: The Lead Architect is currently 'broad-fronted' across every active integration, acting as a bottleneck. Mitigation efforts include decentralizing technical leads for HousingDB and FinAidSys (#4, #6).
- Delay to 2027 NextGenSIS launch: Current prioritization of 100% legacy alignment and historical logic error fixing will delay the foundational build-out for the NextGenSIS launch.
- **Architectural Throughput**: Mitigation efforts now include decentralizing technical leads by pulling resources from HousingDB and FinAidSys to assist with intensive legacy data cleanup.
